title: saxifraga punctata subsp. reniformis
intro: Saxifraga punctata subsp. reniformis, also known as kidney saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family, native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of kidney saxifrage have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and urinary tract problems, and it is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Kidney saxifrage can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and it has been used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall health.
